results.rendered_html:
jīrṇa 1〉 adj. s. u. 1. jar.
— 2〉 m.
a〉 Baum  H. 1114 ; vgl. jarṇa.
— b〉 = jīra, jīraka Kümmel  RĀJAN. _im_ ŚKDR. 
— 3〉 f. ā grober Kümmel  RĀJAN. _im_ ŚKDR. 
— 4〉 n.
a〉 Gebrechlichkeit, Alter: jīrṇamevādhunāṅgeṣu  RĀJA-TAR. 3,316 .
— b〉 Erdharz (śailaja)  RĀJAN. _im_ ŚKDR. 
— Vgl. ajīrṇa.

results.rendered_html:
jīrṇa p. p. [jṝ-kta]
1 Old, ancient.
2 Worn out, ruined, wasted, decayed, tattered (as clothes); vāsāṃsi jīrṇāni yathā vihāya  Bg. 2. 22 ;  U. 6. 38 ;  Māl. 5. 30. 
3 Digested; sujīrṇamannaṃ suvicakṣaṇaḥ sutaḥ  H. 1. 22. 
— rṇaḥ
1 An old man.
2 A tree.
3 Cuminseed.
— ṇā Large cumin-seed.
— rṇaṃ
1 Benzoin.
2 Old age, decrepitude.
Comp.
— uddhāraḥ ‘renewing the old’, repairs, especially of a temple or any charitable or religious institution.
— udyānaṃ ruined or neglected garden.
— jvaraḥ lingering fever.
— parṇaḥ the Kadamba tree.
— vajraṃ a particular gem.
— vastra a. wearing old clothes.
— vāṭikā a ruined house.
